exemplar.title: To what extent does the evolving design of the Barbie doll such as  physical design, body proportions, and racial representation reflect American ideals of female beauty standards, from the 1980s to the present day?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the evolving design of the Barbie doll such as  physical design, body proportions, and racial representation reflect American ideals of female beauty standards, from the 1980s to the present day?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Topic and purpose are clearly stated, with key concepts (body proportions, racial representation) and timeframe defined
Research question is explicitly presented and anchored to the investigationâs scope
Some informed selection of primary and secondary sources is evident
weaknesses: Language is occasionally awkward and digressive, weakening precision
Methodology remains largely descriptive with no systematic explanation of selection/evaluation criteria
Citation style is inconsistent, and justification for chosen decades is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place communication and question clarity in the mid-band (3â4), but methodology is only minimally developed (1â2). Overall performance aligns with a solid mid-band outcome; a score of 3 reflects adequate focus and method with room to strengthen precision and methodological rigor.

strengths: Integration of a variety of relevant sources, including peer-reviewed studies and primary visuals, supports the discussion
Subject knowledge is coherent, summarizing historical shifts and linking them to Barbie lines
Use of a clear visual (Figure 1) enhances understanding and context
weaknesses: Key terms (âbody positivity,â âEurocentricâ) lack clear definitions and are sometimes conflated
Some factual generalizations and inconsistent citation notation undermine academic rigor
Rationale for source selection is often implicit rather than explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Terminology and concepts are mostly accurate (band 3â4), and source application is generally appropriate (band 3â4), but precision and rationale are uneven (band 1â2). The overall knowledge and understanding fit a mid-band performance, justifying a score of 3.

strengths: Demonstrates some relevant research through primary and secondary examples (e.g., Aerie and Dove campaigns)
Includes comparative observations that hint at analytical thinking
Reflective conclusion shows genuine learning progress and evaluation of skills
weaknesses: Source credibility varies and selection criteria are not critically evaluated
Analysis remains predominantly narrative with limited systematic argumentation
Discussion lacks sustained evaluation and counter-perspectives are noted but under-developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research relevance and analysis are adequate with partial consistency (band 4â6), but argument and evaluation remain limited (lower end of band). A holistic score of 4 recognizes pockets of good critical thinking while noting overall superficial depth.

strengths: Follows a conventional structure (abstract, chapters, conclusion, works cited)
Essential formatting elements (title, word count, page numbers) are present
weaknesses: Section numbering and sub-heading labeling are inconsistent
Citation formatting and figure captions are erratic, and some material intrudes into the main text
Tone shifts into first-person commentary, disrupting academic voice
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and layout fall within the lower mid-band (1â2): overall organization is evident but marred by inconsistencies and formatting errors. A score of 2 accurately reflects this level of presentation quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is presented verbatim near the start, is aligned with the topic, and narrows the scope to 1980s-present.  Nonetheless it is long, multi-clausal, and duplicates wording found in the title, suggesting partial rather than full focus.  Later sections occasionally drift (e.g., lengthy discussion of social-media influencers) which shows the connection to discussion is only partially maintained.  These features correspond to a clear but only partially focused question, situated in the 3-4 mark band.

Analysis is largely narrative; the student describes Barbie lines and then states they âreflectâ changing standards without systematically testing the claim.  Comparative moments (e.g., Dolls of the World vs Fashionistas) hint at analysis, but evidence is seldom quantified or critically weighed.  Conclusions are present yet under-supported, indicating limited depth typical of the 1-3 band.

A wide variety of sourcesâfrom peer-reviewed studies (Dittmar et al.) to company press releases and news articlesâare quoted and generally integrated into the narrative.  While some references are superficial or repeated, most have clear relevance to the argument about body image and diversity.  The use is therefore mostly appropriate, though not consistently rigorous, fitting the 3-4 band.

Basic layout elementsâtitle, word count, page numbersâare present, but citation formatting is erratic and figures are inserted without consistent captions or sourcing.  The bibliography repeats entries and includes broken links.  Given these partial but flawed elements, layout is only partially correct, justifying the lower score within the 1-2 band.

The student demonstrates an adequate grasp of content: historical shifts in U.S. beauty ideals are summarised decade by decade and linked to specific Barbie lines.  Knowledge is coherent but sometimes surface-level, with broad generalisations (âBarbie stuck to a narrow ideal for a long timeâ) and occasional factual slips.  Source use supportsâbut does not always deepenâunderstanding, matching the mid-band descriptor.

A conventional structure (abstract, introduction, chapters, conclusion, works cited) is followed, indicating general appropriateness.  Nonetheless, section numbering is inconsistent, and sub-headings sometimes mis-label content (chapter 2 heading differs from table of contents).  Some material (feedback notes) intrudes into main text.  The essay therefore earns the top of the 1-2 band for structure.

The investigation cites many items, but credibility varies (blogs, YouTube reviews).  Selection criteria are not evaluated, making relevance uneven; some sources about 2024 social-media trends have tenuous links to the RQ.  Consequently, research demonstrates minimal but not absent relevance, aligning with the lower band (1-3) and meriting 2 marks.

The student clearly states the general focusâhow Barbieâs changing design reflects American beauty standardsâboth in the title and the opening sentences.  Key concepts such as âbody proportionsâ and âracial representationâ are explicitly introduced and linked to a 1980-present time frame, giving the essay an identifiable purpose.  However, phrasing is sometimes awkward (âpromoting a thin, mostly white ideal of being more inclusiveâ) and occasional digressions weaken precision, so the communication is accurate but not consistently effective.  This aligns best with the mid-band description for adequate rather than exemplary clarity.

The discussion strings together historical description, primary observations and secondary commentary but lacks a sustained, reasoned argument.  Counter-perspectives (e.g., commercial motivation for diversity) are noted briefly without evaluation.  Logical flow is often interrupted by citation errors, so the argument remains limited, matching the 1-3 band at 2 marks.

The methodology section identifies primary sources (specific doll lines, adverts) and secondary academic studies, demonstrating some informed selection.  However, it remains descriptive; there is no explanation of HOW dolls will be examined, why chosen decades are appropriate beyond brief assertions, or criteria for evaluating sourcesâ reliability.  Citation style is inconsistent and many references are popular media rather than scholarly.  Overall, evidence of informed, systematic method is limited, warranting the 1-2 band with the higher score in that range.

Key terms such as âbody positivity,â âEurocentric,â and âmulticulturalismâ are introduced, yet definitions are rarely provided and usage is sometimes inaccurate (e.g., conflating âracial diversityâ and âbody diversityâ).  Citations employ inconsistent notation (<sup><sup>) which indicates limited command of academic conventions.  This places the work in the 1-2 band, with some accurate but generally imprecise terminology.

annotations.comment: The title is presented but uses inconsistent capitalization and is overly wordy. Consider applying sentence-case conventions and simplifying for clarity.
The research question is explicitly stated and aligns with the investigationâs focus from the 1980s to the present. This provides a clear anchor for subsequent analysis.
To improve focus, streamline the research question by removing duplicated phrases and clarifying one key clause to sharpen scope and avoid redundancy.
The word count is correctly provided at the start, confirming compliance with IB requirements. Good attention to this formatting detail.
The table of contents follows a basic structure but shows inconsistent capitalization (e.g., â6.work citedâ) and irregular numbering. Correct these for a professional layout.
The abstract outlines the general topic but is overly descriptive and lacks explicit mention of methodology or key findings. Aim to summarize the approach and results concisely.
Refine the abstract to include specific methods (e.g., comparative visual analysis) and a succinct preview of conclusions to strengthen the overview.
Footnotes display raw HTML entities (e.g., â<sup>â) which disrupt readability. Adopt a consistent citation styleâAPA or MLAâthroughout.
The introduction asserts the timeframe choice but provides limited justification. Elaborate on why the 1980s mark a pivotal shift in American beauty standards.
The sentence ending âHowever, thisâ is incomplete, interrupting the flow. Ensure each paragraph concludes with a full, coherent idea.
The hypothesis statement is clear but broad. Specify the variablesâbody proportions, racial representationâand articulate expected relationships to sharpen focus.
The choice of Dittmar et al. and Inspiring Women series is relevant but lacks rationale. Explain why these sources were selected and how they will inform the analysis.
Analysis of tanning culture in the 1980s is descriptive but could better connect to Barbieâs physical design changes by citing specific doll releases as case studies.
The claim that traditional Barbies negatively impact self-esteem cites two studies but omits details on methodology and sample characteristics. Provide more context to strengthen the argument.
The statement on Barbieâs Fashionistas line making dolls âmore inclusiveâ needs supporting dataâcite survey results or sales figures to demonstrate impact.
Footnotes on this page continue to show HTML markup and inconsistent formatting. Standardize superscript and reference listing in one consistent style.
The section on Christie and Teresa effectively illustrates racial representation, but could discuss why body uniformity persisted despite ethnic diversification.

The social media trends discussion on influencers and adverts is interesting but only tangentially related to Barbie design. Explicitly link these trends back to Barbieâs marketing or design decisions.
Figure 1 is a clear visual of the Dolls of the World line and enhances primary-source analysis through its caption and contextual description.
The caption for Figure 2 should specify the year range and source URL more precisely to reinforce credibility and enable verification.
The inclusion of Aerie and Dove campaigns provides strong secondary evidence of broader body-positive movements that parallel Barbieâs evolution.
Discussion of anti-aging products could be deepened by contrasting this trend with the expanding body-positive lines, highlighting tension in contemporary standards.
The first-person commentary (âIt's really excitingâ¦â) in this section shifts tone. Maintain a formal academic voice for consistency.
Reflecting on refining the research question demonstrates thoughtful engagement and shows the studentâs ability to respond to feedback and narrow scope effectively.
The reflection in the conclusion on how analytical and citation skills improved is evaluative and shows genuine learning progress.
